compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan-review remediation (PASS, advisories triaged):

1. [ac-quality / P6 — valid] 'clean pass' made measurable: a CLEAN shadow pass = a pass whose `baseline_shadow_check` log records `divergent == 0` (no per-binding baseline diverges from its prev_snapshot-derived value over the 5 mirrored fields). The Phase-3 consumer swap is gated on >= 10 CONSECUTIVE such clean passes; any `baseline_shadow_divergence` event resets the counter to 0.

2. [indeterminate / E2 — valid] The 7 known drift instances are enumerated (from the 2026-07-03 derisk census on the epic):
   - 5x archived->Done (class A / TERMINAL_TRANSITION): REB-464, REB-456, REB-465, REB-466, REB-457
   - 1x GC-after-grace (class C / RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE): f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530
   - 1x adopt (class B / ADOPT): REB-532
   Phase 4's capped live heal targets exactly this set; next-pass census must show 0 acting decisions over it.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Progress + honest residual (session 2026-07-03).

LANDED ON MAIN (Gerrit 256):
- Phase 1 baseline dual-write SHADOW — new baseline_shadow.py; when reconciler.baseline_dual_write=true, every persisting pass advances a per-binding baseline from curr WITHOUT any consumer reading it, logging baseline_shadow_check {equal,divergent,seeded} vs prev_snapshot and baseline_shadow_divergence on M>0. Wired into reconcile._persist_and_log before save(); flag off by default; fail-open. Tests: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py.
- Rollback procedure + the workflow_dispatch mode brake + circuit breaker + dual-write shadow documented in docs/jira-sync-setup.md (## Convergence rollout & rollback).
- Circuit breaker (check_blast_radius, default 0.10) is already ENFORCED before the first acting mutation for the level-triggered walk's decisions (terminal-transition / retire / adopt) — landed with 249/253; refuses a mass-retire/mass-transition/mass-adopt pass.

REMAINING CODE (safe to do, not yet done):
- Phase 3 consumer swap: re-point outbound_fields._local_matches_prev from prev_snapshot to BindingStore.get_baseline behind a flag, preserving absent-baseline->local-wins + corrupt-store fail-closed + direction arbitration byte-for-byte, with the RED direction-preservation property test against the swapped consumer. Deliberately NOT flipped: the epic's own risk plan requires >=10 clean shadow passes first.
- decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode (report + acting) + classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase 2 breadth beyond the walk's per-pass census).

REMAINING OPERATIONAL (CANNOT run in an agent session — needs live production Jira + the deployed 20-min workflow):
- >=10 consecutive clean live shadow passes (baseline_shadow_check divergent==0), ~3.5h wall clock, before flipping the Phase-3 consumer.
- Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict of the 7 known drift instances (REB-464/456/465/466/457 terminal, f8b5->REB-530 GC, REB-532 adopt); assert next-pass census = 0 acting.

This ticket stays in_progress: the detection + heals for classes A/B/C/D are live on main and their tickets are closed+signed, and the safe rollout derisk (dual-write shadow + rollback controls) is landed, but the consumer-swap flip and the live-pass validation are the operational tail owned by the deployed reconcile-bridge workflow.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Convergence rollout for epic 3006-e198: land the SAFE derisk code for the four drift-class heals so the reconciler can be rolled out level-triggered without changing arbitration. Deployment context: `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` runs `rebar reconcile --mode live` every 20 min from main; the workflow_dispatch `mode` input is the rollback brake (pin to dry-run).

## Scope (RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03)
This ticket's acceptance is the **in-repo code deliverable** — the derisk mechanism (baseline dual-write shadow), the circuit-breaker enforcement in the level-triggered apply path, and the rollback documentation — all landed on main and unit-tested. The **operational tail** (>= 10 clean live shadow passes, the Phase-3 prev_snapshot->baseline consumer-flag flip, and the capped live heal of the 7 known drift instances) requires live production Jira and the deployed 20-min workflow, so it CANNOT run in an agent session; it is carved out to follow-up **lofty-beech-twang (a118)** and owned by the deployed-workflow operator. Design for the full rollout (Phases 1-4, ADRs 0026-0029) is retained on a118.

## What landed on main (this ticket)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/baseline_shadow.py`: Phase-1 baseline dual-write SHADOW behind `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` — advances a per-binding baseline every pass and logs `baseline_shadow_check` (equal/divergent/seeded over 5 mirrored fields), emitting `baseline_shadow_divergence` on any mismatch, consumed by no one (arbitration unchanged). Wired in `reconcile.py` `_persist_and_log`.
- Circuit breaker `check_blast_radius` (config `reconciler.max_acting_fraction`, default 0.10) enforced in `binding_walk.py` BEFORE the first acting mutation; refuses the whole pass and emits a bridge alert when the acting fraction is exceeded.
- Rollback procedure documented in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` (workflow mode brake, breaker, dual-write shadow).
- Unit tests: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py` (5 cells), breaker-refuse cells in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_binding_walk.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline dual-write shadow behind `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` emits per-pass `baseline_shadow_check` logging and `baseline_shadow_divergence` on divergence, consumes no arbitration input (prev_snapshot untouched); covered by `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_baseline_shadow.py`. — landed on main.
- [ ] Circuit breaker enforced in the level-triggered apply path (`binding_walk.py`): a pass whose acting fraction exceeds `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) refuses BEFORE the first mutation and emits an alert; covered by the breaker-refuse cells in `test_binding_walk.py`. — landed on main.
- [ ] Rollback procedure (pin workflow `mode` input to dry-run) documented in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`. — landed on main.
- [ ] The operational tail (>= 10 clean live shadow passes, Phase-3 consumer-flag flip, capped live heal of the 7 known instances, next-pass census = 0) is carved to follow-up lofty-beech-twang (a118) with full design preserved. — done.
compiled_state.title: Convergence rollout: dual-write shadow, census/divergence alerting, prev_snapshot->baseline consumer swap, breaker wiring, capped live heal
